title: toxicodendron divaricatum
intro: Toxicodendron divaricatum, also known as the yellow lacquer tree or poison sumac, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family, producing a toxic sap that contains urushiol, a potent allergenic and irritant compound. The medicinal properties of Toxicodendron divaricatum lie in its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Toxicodendron divaricatum can be made into a paste or ointment, and it is often used in traditional medicine, although caution should be taken due to its toxic nature and potential allergic reactions.
